title: Global Trade
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
In the second week of February last year, Doric's Weekly Insight focused on the abrupt escalation in global trade tensions following the announcement by U.S. President of a sweeping 25 percent tariff on steel and aluminium imports, targeting approximately 50 billion dollars of trade. The measure was set to affect a broad range of suppliers, with Canada, Brazil and Mexico accounting for nearly half of U.S.
